incidents.vector: Exploited two Cisco ASA WebVPN zero-days (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362) and planted the FIRESTARTER backdoor plus Line Viper malware to retain access to Firepower appliances.
incidents.impact: Attackers regained access to the Cisco firewall in March 2026 without re-exploitation and harvested admin credentials, certificates, and private keys; CISA issued Emergency Directive 25-03 to all federal agencies and now ties the activity to the ArcaneDoor UAT-4356 cluster aligned with Chinese state interests.

incidents.victim: Global critical infrastructure and telecom (SOHO routers/IoT)
incidents.vector: Chinese contractor-run botnets of compromised SOHO routers, IoT and NAS devices used as covert traversal networks to disguise reconnaissance, malware delivery, and data theft.
incidents.impact: A 13-country advisory led by NCSC-UK, CISA, NSA, FBI, ASD, CSE, BSI and others warned that Chinese information-security firms now operate and lease these covert networks (e.g., Raptor Train, KV Botnet) to groups pre-positioning on US critical infrastructure and conducting espionage globally.

incidents.vector: GRU unit exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers to rewrite DNS settings, silently redirecting targets to attacker servers that captured Microsoft OAuth authentication tokens.
incidents.impact: Black Lotus Labs and Microsoft say the campaign ensnared more than 18,000 routers at peak, affecting 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices (foreign ministries, law enforcement, email providers) without deploying malware.

incidents.vector: Exploited a protocol vulnerability to withdraw funds denominated in rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens from the DeFi platform.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292–300 million stolen, the largest crypto heist of the year so far; about $175 million is being laundered through THORChain, Umbra and BitTorrent mixers per Cyvers.

incidents.vector: Infostealer infection on a Context.ai employee's device (Feb 17) stole OAuth tokens; attackers then pivoted into a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account via the Context.ai browser-extension AI tool.
incidents.impact: Attackers accessed Vercel production environments and non-sensitive environment variables; a ShinyHunters-linked actor demanded a $2M ransom, customers were told to rotate all credentials, and Mandiant is leading the investigation.

incidents.victim: MSPs, dental software vendor and downstream customers
incidents.vector: Unauthenticated RCE in BeyondTrust/Bomgar Remote Support (CVE-2026-1731) exploited to take over RMM servers, add admin accounts, and deploy LockBit 3.0 or drop AnyDesk/Atera for persistence.
incidents.impact: Huntress observed five attacks in two weeks; one on April 3 hit a dental software firm and three downstream clients, another on April 15 forced mass isolation of 78 businesses and compromise of four downstream customers with LockBit detonation in several cases.

incidents.victim: TeamPCP supply-chain victims (LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy users)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP used stolen GitHub credentials to push a credential-harvesting payload into LiteLLM (97M monthly downloads) and poison two Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows after earlier compromising Aqua Security's Trivy infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Cloud credentials, secrets, and AI API keys were exfiltrated from countless downstream environments; the group is threatening to leak over 300 GB of stolen data and claims ransomware collaboration with Lapsus$.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack on Friday; city took its IT network offline and surrounding Leon County disconnected from it in a pattern consistent with ransomware containment.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services remained unavailable for days; officials have not confirmed a ransomware strain.

incidents.vector: Attackers harvested records from McGraw Hill's Salesforce instance and, after a failed extortion attempt, published the dataset.
incidents.impact: Personal details of 13.5 million users — names, home addresses, emails, phone numbers — leaked by ShinyHunters.

incidents.vector: Attackers prepared the heist by seeding fake token pools on the platform for days, then exploited vulnerabilities in the margin trading feature to drain funds through the rigged pools.
incidents.impact: $18.4 million in crypto assets stolen from the DeFi platform.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian General Prosecutor's Office and allied militaries
incidents.vector: GRU unit compromised the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors and extended the campaign to military organizations in Greece, Romania, and Serbia.
incidents.impact: Investigative records and military correspondence were accessed; Ctrl-Alt-Intel and Reuters describe it as part of an ongoing cross-border espionage operation.

incidents.vector: Targeted ICS/OT malware dubbed ZionSiphon attempted to find operator software in Israeli networks and push water-pump pressure and chlorine dosing to dangerous settings.
incidents.impact: Darktrace found the malware included politically motivated strings referencing Tel Aviv and Haifa but failed to activate due to a XOR bug in its geolocation check, causing it to self-delete; attribution leans toward Iranian actors.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into the exchange's hot wallets; Grinex publicly blamed "Western intelligence agencies."
incidents.impact: About $13 million (1 billion rubles) stolen; Grinex — a US-sanctioned rebrand of Russian exchange Garantex — shuttered operations after the heist.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated distributed denial-of-service attack against Bluesky's infrastructure beginning late April 15.
incidents.impact: Platform operated intermittently for nearly a day; follow-on DDoS attempts through the week were mitigated.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion affecting the company's IT systems.
incidents.impact: Operations disrupted across Europe and Australia; recovery still under way.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into an internal network hosting customer data; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: South Africa's largest bank confirmed the breach — the third reported hack of a South African bank this year — but has not disclosed scope of customer exposure.

incidents.vector: Compromise of the Zerion crypto-wallet provider attributed publicly to North Korean actors.
incidents.impact: Approximately $100,000 in user assets stolen; Zerion has begun notifying affected customers.
